name: MCPsvr
what_it_does: MCP server registry and marketplace by the creator of 5ire AI client. Provides a curated directory of MCP servers where developers can publish their servers and users can discover, install, and manage MCP server integrations. Enables browsing and installing MCP servers from a central registry rather than manually configuring each one.
use_cases: Discovering available MCP servers from a curated registry
Installing and managing MCP server integrations from a central directory
Publishing custom MCP servers to share with the community
Browsing MCP servers by category or capability from agent planning workflows
not_for: Running MCP server logic directly (this is a registry/marketplace layer)
Teams needing private internal MCP server registries
Production MCP server hosting (use individual server repos for that)
best_when: A developer wants to discover and install MCP servers from a curated marketplace rather than manually searching GitHub — especially useful when using 5ire or other MCP-compatible clients.
avoid_when: You already know which specific MCP servers you need and how to configure them — the registry layer adds overhead over direct installation.

auth.notes: No authentication required for browsing registry. Publishing servers may require account registration on mcpsvr.com.

pricing.notes: Open source registry project by nanbingxyz (creator of 5ire AI client). Free to use.

performance.rate_limits: No known rate limits for registry browsing

known_agent_gotchas: Registry is community-curated — server quality varies; verify individual servers before use
Registry index may lag behind actual MCP ecosystem growth
Installing from registry still requires manual MCP client configuration
From the creator of 5ire — best integrated with 5ire client but works with others
agent_readiness.security_notes: Registry metadata only — no code execution. Individual server security varies. Evaluate each server separately before installing.

security_score_components.security_notes: Registry layer only. No credentials. Individual installed servers have their own security postures.
